title: IMF's Christine Lagarde on Economic Conditions
summary: Nick Burns writes that he has been impressed for several years now by Lagarde’s leadership qualities and her professional and effective management of the IMF. She is clearly a strategic thinker, is articulate and unafraid to take on difficult questions. She is also most definitely a listener who connects well with her audience.
body: Professor Nicholas Burns reflects on Lagarde's statements on economics, climate change and female empowerment. The latest from Belfer See more October 12, 2017 Christine Lagarde , managing director of the International Monetary Fund , spoke about economics, climate change, and female empowerment during an appearance last week (Oct. 5) at Harvard Kennedy School. The event in the JFK Jr. Forum was hosted by Lawrence H. Summers , the Charles W. Eliot University Professor and President Emeritus, and Nicholas Burns , the Roy and Barbara Goodman Family Professor of the Practice of Diplomacy and International Relations. Professor Burns provides his thoughts about the IMF leader’s visit below. FROM PROFESSOR BURNS Professor Larry Summers and I were very pleased to host IMF Managing Director Christine Lagarde on October 5 at the Kennedy School. We have created a new program on economic diplomacy to highlight its vital importance in the 21st century. Lagarde was our first visitor. She will be followed by former Secretary of Commerce Penny Pritzker in late October and former U.S. Trade Representative, Michael Froman in early 2018. What struck me most about Lagarde’s prepared speech and subsequent conversation with our students was her contention that, in addition to economic growth and reform, traditional areas for the IMF to encourage and monitor, she sees a compelling need for governments to focus on climate change, the empowerment of women, the future of work and on corruption. I asked her about this in the Forum, saying it was unusual for an IMF Managing Director to stray from the central focus of the economy in one of her major public speeches of the year. She stuck to her guns in arguing that these additional challenges such as climate change will have an undoubted impact on the global economy. I have been impressed for several years now by Lagarde’s leadership qualities and her professional and effective management of the IMF. She is clearly a strategic thinker, is articulate and unafraid to take on difficult questions. She is also most definitely a listener who connects well with her audience. At dinner afterwards, I mused that if the 20 most powerful leaders in the world today were asked to choose who among them is most respected by the entire group they might very well conclude in a private poll that two women—Lagarde and German Chancellor Angela Merkel—are the world’s most trusted leaders. Our students can certainly learn a lot from her careers in law, politics and international economics and from her combination of intelligence, discipline and commitment to the greater public good. We were delighted to welcome her to Harvard as our first Klein Fellow. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: In October 2017, IMF Managing Director Christine Lagarde spoke at Harvard Kennedy School, hosted by Professors Lawrence Summers and Nicholas Burns. Professor Burns reflected on her visit, noting her emphasis on expanding the IMF's traditional focus beyond economic growth and reform. Lagarde argued that governments must also address climate change, female empowerment, the future of work, and corruption, as these issues significantly impact the global economy. Burns praised Lagarde's leadership, strategic thinking, and ability to connect with audiences, suggesting she is among the world's most respected leaders alongside Angela Merkel. Her visit inaugurated a new economic diplomacy program, with subsequent speakers planned.
result.main_content_zh: 2017年10月，国际货币基金组织（IMF）总裁克里斯蒂娜·拉加德在哈佛大学肯尼迪学院发表演讲，由劳伦斯·萨默斯和尼古拉斯·伯恩斯教授主持。伯恩斯教授回顾了此次访问，指出拉加德主张IMF应超越传统的经济增长与改革关注点。她强调各国政府必须应对气候变化、女性赋权、未来工作形态以及腐败问题，因为这些议题对全球经济具有重大影响。伯恩斯赞扬了拉加德的领导力、战略思维以及与听众的沟通能力，认为她与德国总理安格拉·默克尔同为全球最受尊敬的领导人之一。此次访问标志着一个新的经济外交项目的启动，后续已安排其他演讲者。
